pages.full_text:
We had a lovely relaxed day at this campsite, which is one of the nicest we have ever been to. Lots of shade. Huggledy juggledy. John beat Eve at Scrabble in the morning Eve beat John in the evening. Charles practised shuffling cards. In the afternoon the boys swam while Simon & Eve walked around the campsite. It's pretty huge - 68 sites at least. 2 more wash places. One shower takes wheelchairs, another is reserved for children. The owners seem to like children. They have lots of puzzle games & books in the bar. In one of the books I found "myrtles" means blue berries. I also learned about chestnuts & silk works.
	On our walk we also found a little stone room with a stone domed roof, big enough for 8 people to squat in, made in a terraced wall, hidden.
pages.notes: Text continues from the previous page (begins mid-narrative). No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner. 'Huggledy juggledy' transcribed verbatim — likely a family variant of 'higgledy-piggledy' meaning disorganised/random. The word 'shuffling' appears below a superscript annotation and the word 'cards' is written below 'shuffling', suggesting 'Charles practised shuffling / cards' is a two-line insert. 'myrtles' is in quotation marks in the original. Context (preceding pages B_0032–B_0033) places this entry in the Ariège region of southern France, August 2004, near Ax-les-Thermes. The stone domed room in a terraced wall may refer to a traditional Ariège stone shepherd's shelter (cabane or orri).

pages.full_text:
We swam, had tea & drove into __Vornet__ to look around & have supper. Eve bought a sun dress for Flora in Australia. Simon bought wine & food. John bought a wasp trap.
	We traced where the river had changed course in 1940. The cafe where we had supper was pleasant, out on the square, but one harassed man was doing all the work. There were 2 men with hunchbacks & I wondered if they were coming to take the waters. One of them was English. We went inside the Portugese Hotel & walked in the park. Elegant.
__Thursday 12th August__
	We got up earlier than usual & just managed to walk up the steep slopes to [?St. Martin's] Abbey in the shade. Any later & walkers were toasted. Charles stayed at the van (9.30 - 11.45.) The way was up roads used by 4x4 Landrovers driving people to the top. Lots of chestnut &
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 33 visible in the top-right corner. 'Vornet' is underlined in the original and transcribed verbatim; this may be a small village near Ax-les-Thermes in the Ariège, France. The reference to a river changing course in 1940 and thermal baths ('take the waters') is consistent with the Ax-les-Thermes / Ariège area. 'Portugese Hotel' is the diary's spelling (sic; standard: Portuguese). The abbey name before 'Abbey' reads most likely as 'St. Martin's' but the handwriting is not fully clear — could also be 'St. Martin' or another saint's name. Year 2004 inferred from B_0032_R which explicitly states 'Tuesday August 10th 2004'; Thursday 12th August fits that year perfectly. 'Flora in Australia' refers to a person named Flora living in Australia. Text ends mid-sentence; continues on next spread.
